title: The Murder at the Vicarage
description: The Murder at the Vicarage — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Murder at the Vicarage" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Affair
memes.description: The extramarital affair between Anne Protheroe and Lawrence Redding — the romantic scandal at the heart of The Murder at the Vicarage, providing the most obvious motive for murder.

memes.label: Alibi
memes.description: A defense based on being elsewhere at the time of a crime — a central puzzle in the murder of Colonel Protheroe

memes.label: Anne Protheroe
memes.description: The young, unhappy wife of Colonel Protheroe, whose love affair with Lawrence Redding makes her a prime suspect in her husband's murder

memes.label: Anonymous Letter
memes.description: Cruel, unsigned communications that play a key role in the later stages of the Protheroe murder investigation

memes.label: Arrest
memes.description: The formal act of taking a suspect into police custody — a turning point in the investigation that shifts suspicion from one person to another

memes.label: Blackmail
memes.description: A suspected motive in the murder of Colonel Protheroe, centering on the mysterious Mrs. Lestrange

memes.label: "Chapter 1"
memes.description: "It is difficult to know quite where to begin this story, but I have fixed my choice on a certain Wednesday at luncheon at the Vicarage."

memes.label: "Chapter 2"
memes.description: "Griselda is a very irritating woman."

memes.label: "Chapter 3"
memes.description: "“Nasty old cat,” said Griselda, as soon as the door was…"

memes.label: "Chapter 4"
memes.description: "I had entirely forgotten that we had asked Lawrence Redding to dinner that night."

memes.label: "Chapter 5"
memes.description: "It was nearer seven than half-past six when I approached the Vicarage gate on my return."

memes.label: "Chapter 6"
memes.description: "We puzzled over the business of the clock for some time, but we could make nothing of it."

memes.label: "Chapter 7"
memes.description: "Colonel Melchett is a dapper little man with a habit of snorting suddenly and unexpectedly."

memes.label: "Chapter 8"
memes.description: "We were rather silent on our way down to the police station."

memes.label: "Chapter 9"
memes.description: "After leaving a message at the police station, the Chief Constable announced his intention of paying a visit to Miss…"

memes.label: "Chapter 10"
memes.description: "His remarks on the subject of Miss Marple as we left the house were far from…"

memes.label: "Chapter 11"
memes.description: "I saw at a glance that Colonel Melchett and Inspector Slack had not been seeing eye to eye about the case."

memes.label: "Chapter 12"
memes.description: "I was summoned to the study when Lawrence Redding arrived."

memes.label: "Chapter 13"
memes.description: "I hardly thought it likely that Mrs. Price Ridley had anything so dramatic in view, but I did wonder what had taken her to the police station."

memes.label: "Chapter 14"
memes.description: "On my way home, I ran into Miss Hartnell and she detained me at least ten minutes, declaiming in her deep bass voice against the improvidence and ungratefulness of the lower classes."

memes.label: "Chapter 15"
memes.description: "Hawes’s appearance distressed me very much."

memes.label: "Chapter 16"
memes.description: "As I went out I ran into Haydock on the doorstep."

memes.label: "Chapter 17"
memes.description: "Inspector Slack came round to see me the following morning."

memes.label: "Chapter 18"
memes.description: "The inquest was held that afternoon (Saturday) at two o’clock at the Blue Boar."

memes.label: "Chapter 19"
memes.description: "“Very glad to have met you,” said Lawrence."

memes.label: "Chapter 20"
memes.description: "When I got back to the Vicarage I found that we were in the middle of a domestic…"

memes.label: "Chapter 21"
memes.description: "I cannot say that I have at any time had a great admiration for Mr. Raymond West."

memes.label: "Chapter 22"
memes.description: "Inspector Slack’s orders, once I had got him on the telephone, were brief and emphatic."

memes.label: "Chapter 23"
memes.description: "On the way back, I proposed to Griselda that we should make a detour and go round by the barrow."

memes.label: "Chapter 24"
memes.description: "I returned to the Vicarage to find Hawes waiting for me in my study."

memes.label: "Chapter 25"
memes.description: "I found it hard to shake off the impression left by the anonymous letter."

memes.label: "Chapter 26"
memes.description: "I was in a strange mood when I mounted the pulpit that…"

memes.label: "Chapter 27"
memes.description: "Griselda and Dennis had not yet returned."

memes.label: "Chapter 28"
memes.description: "I hurried down the village street."

memes.label: "Chapter 29"
memes.description: "I don’t know how long I sat there—only a few minutes in reality, I suppose."

memes.label: "Chapter 30"
memes.description: "We stared at her."

memes.label: "Chapter 31"
memes.description: "Colonel Melchett and I both stared at…"

memes.label: "Chapter 32"
memes.description: "There is little more to be told."

memes.label: Church of England
memes.description: The established church in England — the institutional framework of St. Mary Mead's parish life and the professional context of Mr. Clement and Hawes

memes.label: Churchwarden
memes.description: A lay office in the Church of England — the role held by Colonel Protheroe and a vehicle for the novel's theme of hidden double lives

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: The rigid social hierarchy of St. Mary Mead — a structure of class distinctions that shapes behaviour, determines allegiances, and influences the investigation

memes.label: Colonel Melchett
memes.description: The Chief Constable of the county who oversees the investigation into Colonel Protheroe's murder — a well-meaning but unimaginative authority figure

memes.label: Colonel Protheroe
memes.description: The murder victim — a retired army colonel whose authoritarian personality, rigid moralism, and talent for making enemies ensured that nearly everyone in St. Mary Mead had a reason to want him dead

memes.label: Confession
memes.description: A formal admission of guilt — a central plot device in The Murder at the Vicarage, where the first confession proves false

memes.label: Coroner
memes.description: The official who presides over the inquest into Colonel Protheroe's death — the legal mechanism through which the cause of death is formally established and the investigation is launched

memes.label: Dennis
memes.description: The vicar's young nephew, whose adolescent enthusiasm for detective fiction and irreverent observations provide comic relief and occasional insight

memes.label: Dr. Haydock
memes.description: The village doctor whose medical expertise and personal loyalties place him at the intersection of professional duty and private knowledge

memes.label: Dr. Stone
memes.description: A visiting archaeologist excavating a barrow on Protheroe's land — an outsider whose mysterious activities make him an object of suspicion

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: The sense of moral and social obligation that motivates and constrains the characters of St. Mary Mead

memes.label: Evidence
memes.description: The body of facts and testimony gathered during the murder investigation — and the novel's demonstration that evidence can mislead as readily as it can illuminate

memes.label: Funeral
memes.description: The burial of Colonel Protheroe — a communal ritual that brings the village together, reveals family tensions, and marks the transition from shock to mourning

memes.label: Gossip
memes.description: The informal information network of St. Mary Mead that both complicates and assists the murder investigation

memes.label: Griselda Clement
memes.description: The young, irreverent wife of the vicar whose sharp wit and casual observations contribute significantly to the investigation

memes.label: Hawes
memes.description: The new curate with High Church views, whose troubled conscience and connection to the anonymous letters make him a key figure in the investigation's final stages

memes.label: Inspector Slack
memes.description: The police detective assigned to Colonel Protheroe's murder — efficient, unimaginative, and thoroughly wrong about nearly everything

memes.label: Interview
memes.description: The formal questioning of witnesses and suspects by the police — the primary mechanism through which the investigation unfolds and secrets are revealed

memes.label: Justice
memes.description: The moral and legal concept of justice as it manifests in the investigation and resolution of Colonel Protheroe's murder

memes.label: Lawrence Redding
memes.description: A young artist whose love affair with Anne Protheroe and dramatic confession make him the primary suspect in Colonel Protheroe's murder

memes.label: Lawrence Redding's Studio
memes.description: The artist's studio where Lawrence Redding paints — and where his secret affair with Anne Protheroe is discovered

memes.label: Letter
memes.description: Written correspondence as a crucial plot device in The Murder at the Vicarage — the anonymous letter accusing Colonel Protheroe, the forged unfinished letter, and letters as police evidence.

memes.label: Lettice Protheroe
memes.description: Colonel Protheroe's daughter from his first marriage — a provocative young woman whose strained family relationships add to the pool of potential motives

memes.label: Love
memes.description: Romantic love as a driving motivation in The Murder at the Vicarage — the affair between Anne Protheroe and Lawrence Redding, and the complexities of love as both alibi and suspicion.

memes.label: Mary
memes.description: The vicarage housemaid whose discovery of Colonel Protheroe's body sets the investigation in motion

memes.label: Miss Cram
memes.description: Dr. Stone's secretary, whose unconfirmed alibi of being at the barrow at the time of the murder places her among the final suspects

memes.label: Miss Hartnell
memes.description: A prominent village gossip and parish worker whose self-righteousness and intrusive observation provide both comic relief and occasional useful information

memes.label: Miss Marple
memes.description: An elderly spinster of St. Mary Mead whose encyclopedic knowledge of village life and human nature makes her the novel's true detective — Agatha Christie's first and most enduring series character, introduced in this 1930 debut

memes.label: Miss Wetherby
memes.description: A St. Mary Mead resident described as "a mixture of vinegar and gush" — sharp-tongued yet sentimentally susceptible, a fixture of village society

memes.label: Motive
memes.description: The psychological reason that drives a person to commit a crime — the central question in the investigation of Colonel Protheroe's murder, and a concept the novel complicates by showing that the strongest motives are not always the most obvious ones

memes.label: Mr. Clement
memes.description: The vicar of St. Mary Mead and first-person narrator of the novel, who becomes an amateur detective when a murder occurs in his study

memes.label: Mrs. Archer
memes.description: An elderly cleaning woman at the Vicarage whose testimony about the pistol's whereabouts becomes a key point of evidence in the murder investigation

memes.label: Mrs. Lestrange
memes.description: A mysterious woman who arrives in St. Mary Mead under an assumed name — the object of Inspector Slack's blackmail theory and the keeper of the novel's deepest secret

memes.label: Mrs. Pratt
memes.description: The cook at Old Hall, Colonel Protheroe's estate, referenced by Mary the vicarage maid.

memes.label: Mrs. Price Ridley
memes.description: A devout and relentlessly gossipy member of the Vicarage congregation, whose lost pound note provokes a conversation that triggers the novel's opening crisis

memes.label: Mrs. Sadler
memes.description: Hawes the curate's landlady in St. Mary Mead, who discovered his suicide attempt.

memes.label: Much Benham
memes.description: The adjoining market town two miles from St. Mary Mead — home to the police station, hospital, and various shops that villagers frequent

memes.label: Much Benham Police
memes.description: The police force responsible for investigating Colonel Protheroe's murder — the official apparatus of law enforcement that proves unable to solve the crime without amateur assistance

memes.label: Old Hall
memes.description: The Protheroe family home — an imposing house on the outskirts of St. Mary Mead whose elegant rooms conceal the domestic misery that provides the novel's most visible motive for murder

memes.label: Parish
memes.description: The ecclesiastical community of St. Mary Mead — the social and administrative context within which the murder investigation unfolds

memes.label: Pistol
memes.description: The Mauser .25 pistol — the murder weapon used to kill Colonel Protheroe and a key piece of physical evidence

memes.label: Much Benham Police
memes.description: The Much Benham police force, led by Inspector Slack and represented locally by Constable Hurst — the official investigators of Colonel Protheroe's murder, whose professional methods are contrasted with Miss Marple's intuitive approach

memes.label: Raymond West
memes.description: Miss Marple's nephew, a successful author whose intellectual pretensions are gently satirized by both his aunt and the narrator

memes.label: Secret
memes.description: Hidden knowledge and concealed truths that drive the plot of The Murder at the Vicarage

memes.label: Sermon
memes.description: The vicar's weekly duty of sermon-writing — a narrative motif that frames the novel's opening and reflects on the tension between spiritual calling and worldly distraction

memes.label: Servant Life
memes.description: The domestic servant class in St. Mary Mead, forming a parallel social network of gossip, observation, and class tension beneath the surface of village investigations.

memes.label: Silence
memes.description: Silence as a form of communication and concealment in The Murder at the Vicarage — what characters refuse to say is often as revealing as what they do say.

memes.label: St. Mary Mead
memes.description: The fictional English village where the novel is set — a quiet community whose placid surface conceals murder, secrets, and the keen observation of Miss Marple

memes.label: Suspicion
memes.description: The state of being suspected or suspecting others — a pervasive atmosphere in St. Mary Mead that shapes the investigation and reveals character

memes.label: Telephone
memes.description: The telephone as a critical plot device in the investigation, used for anonymous calls, alibi verification, and communication among characters.

memes.label: The Barrow
memes.description: Dr. Stone's archaeological excavation on Colonel Protheroe's land — a running subplot that intersects with the murder investigation

memes.label: The Blue Boar
memes.description: The village inn of St. Mary Mead — a gathering place for villagers and a useful source of information for the investigation

memes.label: St. Mary Mead Parish Church
memes.description: The parish church of St. Mary Mead, where the vicar serves — a focal point of village life and the scene of church decoration disputes, parish meetings, and a dramatic funeral sermon

memes.label: Lawrence Redding's Confession
memes.description: Lawrence Redding's dramatic false confession — walking into the police station and declaring himself the murderer — is the first major twist in the investigation

memes.label: The Inquest
memes.description: The coroner's inquest into the death of Colonel Protheroe — a formal legal proceeding that gathers witness testimony, establishes the medical facts of the death, and serves as the bridge between informal investigation and formal trial

memes.label: The Murder of Colonel Protheroe
memes.description: The murder of Colonel Protheroe — shot in the vicarage study between 6:20 and 6:35 PM — the event that transforms a quiet English village into a crime scene and reveals that nearly everyone had a motive

memes.label: St. Mary Mead Parish
memes.description: The Church of England parish of St. Mary Mead — the institutional and social framework of village life, governed by the vicar and churchwardens, and the community whose hidden tensions are exposed by Colonel Protheroe's murder

memes.label: The Trial
memes.description: The trial of Lawrence Redding and Anne Protheroe for the murder of Colonel Protheroe — a public legal proceeding that ends in acquittal while the real murderer remains unidentified by the law

memes.label: The Vicarage
memes.description: The home of Mr. Clement and his wife Griselda — and the site of Colonel Protheroe's murder. The novel's primary setting, where the boundary between domestic sanctuary and crime scene is violently erased

memes.label: The Will
memes.description: Colonel Protheroe's last will and testament — a potential source of motive and a key element of the financial dimension of the murder case

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The elusive goal of the murder investigation — a concept that proves more complex than simple factual accuracy, as characters speak truly without telling the truth, and the path to factual truth runs through understanding why people lie

memes.label: Verdict
memes.description: The legal verdict in the investigation of Colonel Protheroe's murder, delivered at the inquest and shaping the subsequent course of the inquiry.

memes.label: Witness
memes.description: Witness testimony as the formal mechanism of evidence-gathering in The Murder at the Vicarage — the inquest witnesses whose accounts shape the official narrative of Colonel Protheroe's murder.
